tasks.title: Task 1: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click on the Open Console button, and you will get redirected to AWS Console in a new browser tab.

On the AWS sign-in page, Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab. Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Leave the Account ID as default. Never edit/remove the 12 digit Account ID present in the AWS Console. otherwise, you cannot proceed with the lab.
Now copy your User Name and Password in the Lab Console to the IAM Username and Password in AWS Console and click on the Sign in button.
Once Signed In to the AWS Management Console, Make the default AWS Region as US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1.
Ensure you are in the US East (N. Virginia) us-east-1 Region to begin launching an EC2 instance in the Amazon cloud.
Navigate to EC2 by clicking on the Services menu in the top, then click on EC2 in the Compute section.
Click on the Instances option on the left panel, and then click on the Launch Instances button.
Name : Enter EC2Server
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances
Select Amazon Linux 2023 AMI from the dropdown.
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances

An instance type in AWS refers to a virtual server configuration that determines the computing resources, such as CPU, memory, and storage, available to an instance. It is the basic building block for creating an EC2 instance in the AWS cloud.
For Instance Type: Select t3.micro
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances
t3.micro is an instance type in AWS that comes with 2 vCPU, and 1GB memory and is suitable for low-traffic web servers, small development environments, and other lightweight applications.

AWS Key pair is a secure pair of keys used for login and access to EC2 instances. It includes a public key placed on the instance and a private key kept on the user's local computer, used for authentication to prevent unauthorized access.
For Key pair(login): Select Create a new key pair Button
Key pair name: WhizKey
Key pair type: RSA
Private key file format: .pem
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances
Click the Edit button in the network settings.
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances

Select the default VPC and a subnet in the us-east-1 region. Enable Auto-assign public IP, choose Create security group, and give name as SSH_SG and description as Security Group for SSH. And leave everything as default.
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances
At the top right, set the Number of instances to 2, then click Launch instance.
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances
After creating the EC2 instances, rename the first instance from EC2server to ‘EC2server-1' and the second instance from 'EC2server' to 'EC2server-2'.
Task 2: Launch an EC2 Instances
In the EC2 sidebar panel, click Volumes to create a new volume.
Task 3 : Create New EBS Volume
Set the following values in the Create volume form:
Volume type: Provisioned IOPS SSD (io1)
Size (GiB): 10
IOPS: 500
Availability Zone: us-east-1a
You must check the box for Enable Multi-Attach before proceeding.
Leave other settings as default, then click Create Volume.
Task 3 : Create New EBS Volume
After creating the volume and EC2 instances:
Now, Select the newly created volume.
Click Actions and then choose Attach volume.
Task 3 : Create New EBS Volume
In the Instance field, choose EC2server-1, choose Device name “ /dev/sdf ” then click Attach.
Repeat the same process to attach the same volume to EC2server-2.
Make sure the volume is in the same Availability Zone (us-east-1a) as both instances.
Task 3 : Create New EBS Volume

tasks.title: Task 4 : Mounting an EBS Volume in Linux

steps.text: Now, Navigate to EC2 Instance, Use EC2 Instance Connect to access and establish a connection between EC2Server-1 and EC2Server-2 via the browser.
Task 4 : Mounting an EBS Volume in Linux
On Both EC2s, Check and Identify the EBS Volume :
Look for the EBS volume. It should show up as follow,
Task 4 : Mounting an EBS Volume in Linux
On EC2Server-1 (Writer) — Format, Mount, Write paste and run the following command:
Task 4 : Mounting an EBS Volume in Linux
On EC2server-2 (Reader) — Mount as Read-Only paste and run the following command:
Task 4 : Mounting an EBS Volume in Linux
Once the lab steps are completed, please click on the Validation button on the Right side panel.
This will validate the resources in the AWS account and shows you whether you have completed this lab successfully or not.
Task 5: Validation of the Lab
You have successfully launched two EC2 Instances
You have successfully created an EBS volume
You have attached the Multi-attach enabled EBS volume to multiple EC2 instances.
You have formatted and mounted the EBS volume on both instances.
You have verified that Instance B can read the file created by Instance A.
Sign out of AWS Account.
You have successfully completed the lab.
Once you completed the steps, click on End Lab from your lab lab console and wait till the process gets completed.
